Hello boys and girls. Dr. Cascio here just dropping by to let you guys know that I like muscles. I actually think we have this in common, which is why I want to share this with you guys and specifically what I want to talk about is maintaining muscle mass during a diet phase. So pretty much everybody can benefit from this sort of information. Let us move along. So I want to let you guys know it actually started with a question in our group and and it's really the question really was raised something like we use fat for energy but when does the body start to burn muscle instead? And follow up question is what supplements would you suggest to prevent this? Okay, and I want to provide an alarming answer to you guys and that it's you are burning protein all the time. You are burning protein all the time. All right. Are you in an energy deficit? Muscle loss. Energy maintenance? Muscle loss. But what about surplus? For sure there's some maintenance and gain going. No muscle loss. Okay, this is alarming. What the hell is going on? Let's find out. Let's find out. So what I want to discuss is the concept of protein turnover. Now protein turnover is the balance between building protein and bringing it down. That's actually about it. So if the balance of the protein turnover is positive, then you are netting a gain. Okay, so you are building more than you are breaking it down. And if the balance is negative, then you are netting a loss. Okay, which means you are breaking it down more than you are building it. So here's stuff that we already know. Getting enough protein helps build muscle. Okay, weight training helps build muscle. Okay, so here's the stuff that may seem strange. Maintaining muscle mass is an illusion. It's a construct. It's a construct. Okay? You're actually just building muscle at the rate that you're losing muscle. This is what matricine balance is, more or less. Okay? So there are two things that build muscle directly. One, grub. Two, and two. Well, I guess there are two things that build muscle directly. One, grub, two, and hormones. Okay, anabolic hormones. And then the stuff that builds muscle indirectly, this is where training comes in. Okay, this is where training comes in. So now we're going to go back to the second part of our original question. What supplements Dr. Cascio, do you suggest to prevent muscle loss? I'm so glad you asked. Okay, the same supplements I suggest to augment muscle gain. Okay, so We are going to be addressing the three things that I talked about a little bit earlier. We're going to be addressing food. We're going to be addressing hormones, specifically anabolic hormones and training. Okay? So we know the three categories direct food and hormones and direct training. Okay? And we just prioritize our supplementation accordingly. So with food, we have procedural abs, protein blend. It's delicious. Okay, on the hormone side, we have test storm and sleep multiplier.
